notes: Twenty-ninth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its published mission says students should be knowledgeable, self-sufficient, responsible, and productive members of society.
Official materials say the school has operated since 1997.
The school describes itself as teacher- and parent-directed and built around individualized needs.
Its educational philosophy says learning should be relevant and active so students become lifelong learners and responsible citizens.
Official staff pages list Heather Lupton as principal.
Board pages list Denise Delamar as board chair.
Its mission says the school empowers all students to reach their highest potential through rigorous and innovative academics.
Official materials describe the school as family-oriented and collaborative.
The school highlights project-based learning and individualized instruction as part of its educational approach.
Official pages say students often work in multi-age groups to build collaboration and social skills.
Staff pages identify Juliana Harris as principal.
Board materials list Rhonda LeViness as board chair.
Its public branding centers on the phrase Learning, Leading, Living.
The school's mission says it prepares learners for successful, meaningful lives as culturally aware and civically engaged adults.
Official materials say the school is guided by the four Cs: communication, collaboration, critical thinking, and creativity.
Its vision says all scholars will learn at high levels and use their education to become lifelong learners and positive global contributors.
Leadership pages list Dr. Cassie Price as chief administrator.
Board pages identify Ashley Carter as board chair.
Its public-facing materials often refer to the school simply as The Point.
The school's mission says it aims to instill a productive mindset while fostering a sense of belonging and a culture of excellence.
Official materials say students participate in annual college tours to campuses inside and outside North Carolina.
The school says families receive weekly progress reports and maintain daily communication with teachers through Class Dojo.
Leadership pages identify Dr. Marilyn Andrews as superintendent and exceptional children's director.
Board pages list Demetria Graham as chairwoman.
Its mission says the school empowers all students to become successful learners who exhibit confidence, social responsibility, and a love of learning.
Official materials say the school is committed to creating community-minded leaders.
The school highlights project-based learning as one of its core academic features.
Official pages say scholars participate in life-skills activities such as gardening, cooking, and financial literacy alongside academics.
Staff pages identify Dr. George Wilhelm as principal.
Board materials list Chad Mitchell as board chair.
